source: Knowledge Graph Embedding by Translating on Hyperplanes

concepts.finding: TransH: knowledge graph embedding for N-ary and reflexive relations. Each relation has a hyperplane (normal vector) and a translation vector on that hyperplane. Addresses TransE limitations with 1-N, N-1, and N-N relations. Outperforms TransE on link prediction and triple classification benchmarks.
concepts.implication: Keyword-intent relationships can involve multiple intents per keyword (N-N relations). TransH models each intent as a hyperplane with keywords projecting differently per intent. Keywords can belong to multiple intents with different "translations."
concepts.open_question: Should keyword-to-intent mapping use TransE-style (one embedding per entity) or TransH-style (relation-specific projections)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A TransH-style model where each intent has a projection hyperplane produces better multi-label keyword-intent assignments than TransE-style embeddings.
